title: quercus salicina var. stenophylloides
intro: Quercus salicina var. stenophylloides, also known as the Chinese corkbark oak, is a variety of the Quercus salicina tree, native to China and other parts of Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the tannins and flavonoids present in its bark, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Quercus salicina var. stenophylloides include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. In traditional Chinese medicine, the bark is often prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or extracts, which can be consumed to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
